Identify all breaking changes and provide the exact fixes.

input.initialPrompt:
Migrate this .NET 8 library and web API to .NET 9. It was compiled against .NET 9 RC2.
The ICache service is only registered as a non-keyed service.
Identify all breaking changes and provide the exact fixes.

data.reasoningText: Let me look at the files in the current directory to understand the codebase and identify breaking changes when migrating from .NET 8 to .NET 9.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-60f8a785762a4ac397b452934c77a6e8 && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null || echo \"dotnet not available\"", "description": "Check dotnet SDK version", "initial_wait": 10}

data.content:
I've identified **4 breaking changes**. Here they are with fixes:

## Breaking Changes

### 1. TFM — `net8.0` → `net9.0`
Trivial but required.

### 2. `[FromKeyedServices("cache")]` on a non-keyed service (ApiController.cs:5)
In .NET 9, resolving a keyed service that was only registered as a **non-keyed** service throws `InvalidOperationException`. Since `ICache` is registered non-keyed, remove the attribute.

### 3. `String.Trim(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` overload removed in .NET 9 GA (StringUtils.cs:11)
The `params ReadOnlySpan<char>` overload existed only in RC2. In GA, `Trim` accepts `char[]`. Convert the span to an array.

### 4. `[InlineArray]` max length now 1 MiB (StringUtils.cs:14)
.NET 9 caps `InlineArray` at 1,048,576 elements. The current value `1048577` exceeds that by 1.

Applying all fixes:

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-60f8a785762a4ac397b452934c77a6e8/ApiController.cs", "old_str": "    public ApiController([FromKeyedServices(\"cache\")] ICache cache) { }", "new_str": "    public ApiController(ICache cache) { }"}

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-60f8a785762a4ac397b452934c77a6e8/StringUtils.cs", "old_str": "        ReadOnlySpan<char> trimChars = [' ', '\\t', '\\n'];\n        return input.Trim(trimChars);", "new_str": "        char[] trimChars = [' ', '\\t', '\\n'];\n        return input.Trim(trimChars);"}

data.content: Now let me also handle the nullable `UserAgent` — in .NET 9 the `HttpListenerRequest.UserAgent` annotation changed to `string?`, so `.Length` on it without a null check will produce a warning/potential NRE:

data.content: Build succeeded with zero warnings and errors. Let me show the final state:

## Summary of Breaking Changes & Fixes

| # | Breaking Change | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **TFM**: `net8.0` → `net9.0` | Updated `MyLib.csproj` |
| 2 | **Keyed DI on non-keyed service**: `[FromKeyedServices("cache")]` throws at runtime in .NET 9 when `ICache` is only registered as non-keyed | Removed the attribute; inject `ICache` directly |
| 3 | **`String.Trim(ReadOnlySpan<char>)` removed in GA**: RC2 had `params ReadOnlySpan<char>` overloads that were cut | Changed to `char[]` which resolves to the existing `Trim(params char[])` |
| 4 | **`[InlineArray]` max size capped at 1 MiB** (1,048,576 elements): `1048577` exceeds the limit | Reduced to `1048576` |
| 5 | **`HttpListenerRequest.UserAgent` now nullable** (`string?`): direct `.Length` is a potential NRE | Added null-conditional `?.Length` |
